steps.title: Isolate the term containing H
steps.content:
From $D = T - \frac{9}{25}(100 - H)$, subtract $T$ from both sides:
$$D - T = -\frac{9}{25}(100 - H)$$

steps.title: Clear the fraction by multiplying
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $-\frac{25}{9}$ to isolate the $(100 - H)$ term:
$$\frac{25}{9}(T - D) = 100 - H$$

steps.title: Isolate H and match the answer
steps.content:
Rearrange to isolate $H$:
$$H = 100 - \frac{25}{9}(T - D)$$
Rewrite by flipping the sign in the parentheses:
$$H = \frac{25}{9}(D - T) + 100$$
This matches choice A — A is correct.
